lede: A look at January 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Princess Beatrice and Maher Magri, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Ram Mandir
events.desc: Ram Mandir is inaugurated by Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi at Ayodhya, Uttar Pradesh after 500 years of dispute.

events.title: Evo Morales
events.desc: Evo Morales is inaugurated as President of Bolivia, becoming the country's first indigenous president.

events.title: Supreme Court of the United States
events.desc: The Supreme Court of the United States delivers its decisions in Roe v. Wade and Doe v. Bolton, legalizing elective abortion in all fifty states. This decision is subsequently overturned in Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization in 2022.

events.title: Boeing 747
events.desc: The Boeing 747, the world's first "jumbo jet", enters commercial service for launch customer Pan American Airways with its maiden voyage from New York's John F. Kennedy International Airport to London Heathrow Airport.

events.title: Central Intelligence Agency
events.desc: Creation of the Central Intelligence Group, forerunner of the Central Intelligence Agency.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: British and Commonwealth troops capture Tobruk from Italian forces during Operation Compass.

events.title: Ramsay MacDonald
events.desc: Ramsay MacDonald becomes the first Labour Prime Minister of the United Kingdom.

events.title: SS Valencia
events.desc: SS Valencia runs aground on rocks on Vancouver Island, British Columbia, killing more than 130.

events.title: Anglo-Zulu War
events.desc: Anglo-Zulu War: the Battle of Isandlwana results in a Zulu victory.

events.title: Convention Parliament (1689)
events.desc: The Convention Parliament convenes to determine whether James II and VII, the last Roman Catholic monarch of England, Ireland and Scotland, had vacated the thrones of England and Ireland when he fled to France in 1688.

births.name: Princess Beatrice
births.line: daughter of Princess Beatrice, born 11th in line of succession to the British throne

deaths.text: James Ford Rhodes, American historian and author
John Carteret, 2nd Earl Granville, English politician, Lord Lieutenant of Ireland

observances.text: Christian feast day: Vincent, Orontius, and Victor
Christian feast day: Blessed William Joseph Chaminade
